methodology.name: Z → A Reverse Extrapolation Pipeline
methodology.description: Define the final, mathematically verifiable output (Z) first, then work backward to deduce all prerequisite data structures, transformations, and validation gates. Inverts traditional forward-chaining approaches.
principles: Output-first: Z defines terminal output schema before any code is written
Design by Contract (DbC): Every agent-to-agent handoff enforces preconditions, postconditions, and invariants via JSON schema validation
Agentic V-Model: Acceptance criteria and test cases are defined BEFORE generative execution begins
LLMs never calculate: Semantic extraction is strictly separated from deterministic calculus
Human-in-the-Loop gates: High-uncertainty outputs require human approval before propagation

timeline.phase_2_target: UAD 3.6 MISMO XML for real estate appraisers (longer-term, higher enterprise value)

meta.changelog: | v1.5.0: Supabase pivot — auth/db/storage integrated, Agent N/O scopes updated

documents.title: Ontology Thinking Mode
documents.summary: Elaborates on the 'Ontology Thinking Mode' and GOAS framework application. Emphasizes a reverse-chaining, goal-oriented approach to problem-solving, the importance of validation, and the need to avoid assumptions. Discusses packaging solutions and the strategic advantage of the UAD 3.6 standard.

key_concepts.name: Ontology Thinking Mode
key_concepts.definition: A cognitive framework that applies ontological analysis to problems — defining entities, their properties, and relationships before attempting solutions.

key_concepts.name: GOAS (Goal-Oriented Action System)
key_concepts.definition: A planning framework that defines goals first, then works backward to identify necessary actions and preconditions. Used in game AI and now applied to data pipeline design.

key_concepts.name: Human API Bottleneck
key_concepts.definition: The core problem: skilled professionals manually translate unstructured data into structured formats, creating latency, cost, and error cascades.

key_concepts.name: Assumption Triage
key_concepts.definition: A protocol for identifying and validating assumptions before building systems. Prevents building on false premises.

key_concepts.name: UAD 3.6 MISMO XML
key_concepts.definition: Uniform Appraisal Dataset version 3.6 using MISMO XML schema. A standardized format for real estate appraisal data required by GSEs (Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac).

entities.name: Xactimate
entities.description: Insurance estimation software producing actuarial damage reports

entities.name: UAD 3.6
entities.description: Uniform Appraisal Dataset v3.6 for real estate appraisals

entities.name: MISMO XML
entities.description: Mortgage Industry Standards Maintenance Organization XML schema

entities.name: Fannie Mae
entities.description: GSE requiring UAD compliance

entities.name: Freddie Mac
entities.description: GSE requiring UAD compliance

requirements.description: System must define output schema (Z) before any implementation
All assumptions must pass triage protocol before being encoded
Solution must be packageable as a product, not just a custom script

findings.finding: The Human API bottleneck exists across multiple industries: construction, legal, real estate, logistics
findings.evidence: Multiple industry verticals analyzed in companion Data Friction Matrix documents

findings.finding: UAD 3.6 compliance represents a higher-value but longer-term market vs roofing CRM integration
findings.evidence: GSE certification requirements create significant barriers to entry but also competitive moats

documents.title: Z → A Pipeline Research & Blueprint
documents.summary: Details the Z→A Pipeline methodology which inverts traditional forward-chaining by defining the final output (Z) first and working backward. Emphasizes rigorous data contracts, verification checkpoints, and exception handling. Draws parallels with backward chaining in AI, Hoare Logic, GOAP, and Spec-Driven Development.

key_concepts.name: Z → A Pipeline (Reverse Extrapolation)
key_concepts.definition: A methodology that starts with the final, mathematically verifiable output (Z) and deduces all prerequisite data structures and transformations backward to the raw input (A).

key_concepts.name: Design by Contract (DbC)
key_concepts.definition: Enforcing formal, verifiable agreements (preconditions, postconditions, invariants) between agents via JSON schema validation and strict data gatekeeping.

key_concepts.name: Agentic V-Model
key_concepts.definition: Adapting the V-Model from systems engineering to LLM pipelines: acceptance criteria and test cases are defined BEFORE generative execution begins.

key_concepts.name: Backward Chaining
key_concepts.definition: Starting from a goal and working backward through rules/preconditions to find what inputs/facts are needed. Analogous to the Z→A approach.

key_concepts.name: Hoare Logic
key_concepts.definition: Formal system for reasoning about program correctness using preconditions and postconditions. Theoretical foundation for the DbC approach.

entities.name: GOAP (Goal-Oriented Action Planning)
entities.description: Game AI planning algorithm that searches backward from goal states

entities.name: Hoare Logic
entities.description: Axiomatic system for program correctness proofs

entities.name: Spec-Driven Development
entities.description: Development approach where specifications drive implementation

requirements.description: Every pipeline node must have defined preconditions and postconditions (DbC)
Verification checkpoints must gate data flow between nodes
Exception handling must be built into every node transition

findings.finding: The Z→A approach aligns with established CS concepts: backward chaining, Hoare Logic, and GOAP
findings.evidence: Direct parallels drawn to formal verification and goal-oriented planning in AI

findings.finding: LLM pipelines benefit from the V-Model approach where tests are designed before implementation
findings.evidence: Applied blueprint demonstrates test-first design for automated video generation pipeline

documents.title: PRD - ScopeBridge
documents.summary: Outlines a phased approach to building the Ontology Engine, detailing specific agent tasks for data acquisition, CRM API research, and core ontology development. Highlights critical risks and blindspots to be addressed in a backlog. Currently incomplete — requires expansion.

key_concepts.name: 4-Node Pipeline Architecture
key_concepts.definition: The core processing pipeline: (1) Ingestion (OCR, PII redaction), (2) Semantic Extraction (LLM, JSON schema), (3) Deterministic Calculus (Python math), (4) Output Routing (CSV/API).

key_concepts.name: Parallel Workstreams
key_concepts.definition: Four concurrent development tracks: Alpha (Ingestion/OCR/Sanitization), Beta (Ontology Core), Gamma (Destination Adapters), Delta (Phase 2 R&D).

key_concepts.name: Risk & Blindspot Backlog
key_concepts.definition: A prioritized list of unknown risks requiring future research and mitigation strategies.

entities.name: Ontology Engine
entities.description: The product name for the Ontology Engine MVP targeting roofing contractors

entities.name: JobNimbus
entities.description: Primary CRM target for Phase 1 roofing integration

entities.name: AccuLynx
entities.description: CRM target with advanced API and supplier integrations

entities.name: Buildertrend
entities.description: CRM target with CSV-based import system

entities.name: Microsoft Presidio
entities.description: Open-source PII detection and anonymization framework

entities.name: Tesseract
entities.description: Open-source OCR engine

entities.name: AWS Textract
entities.description: Cloud-based OCR/document analysis service

requirements.description: PII must be redacted locally BEFORE any data reaches the LLM
LLMs must NEVER perform calculations — only semantic extraction
System must support both native PDF and scanned image inputs via dynamic OCR routing
Output must support CSV (Buildertrend, QBO bridge) and API payloads (AccuLynx)
Token chunking strategy required for large documents exceeding LLM context windows

findings.finding: Roofing/Xactimate is the fastest path to revenue validation of the core architecture
findings.evidence: Most accessible market with clear pain points and existing contractor relationships

findings.finding: UAD 3.6 MISMO XML is Phase 2 due to GSE certification requirements and higher complexity
findings.evidence: Requires partner access to GSE submission systems

documents.title: Roofing CRM & Xactimate Integration Blueprint
documents.summary: Comprehensive technical blueprint for bridging Xactimate data to CRMs (Buildertrend, JobNimbus, AccuLynx). Covers the Lexicon Matrix (50 Xactimate codes), algorithmic conversion models (dimensional analysis, waste factors), and data integration protocols (CSV and REST API). Includes 36 cited sources.

key_concepts.name: Lexicon Matrix
key_concepts.definition: A mapping of ~50 core Xactimate category codes to their semantic meanings, physical units, and supplier equivalents. The foundation for semantic extraction.

key_concepts.name: Roofer Math (Deterministic Calculus)
key_concepts.definition: Industry-specific mathematical conversions: Squares → Bundles (×3), linear feet → pieces (÷10), pitch-adjusted waste factors (steep pitch = 15% waste), and bundle rounding (always round UP).

key_concepts.name: O&P Double-Dip Prevention
key_concepts.definition: Overhead & Profit margins in Xactimate estimates must be stripped before pushing to CRM to prevent double-taxation when the CRM applies its own O&P.

key_concepts.name: CSV Integration Protocol
key_concepts.definition: Structured CSV format for importing data into CRMs, with specific column mappings per CRM (Buildertrend uses assembly imports, JobNimbus uses QBO bridge).

key_concepts.name: REST API Integration Protocol
key_concepts.definition: Direct API payload construction for CRMs with documented APIs. AccuLynx V2 is the most documented; JobNimbus API exists but has limited documentation.

key_concepts.name: Duplication Paradox
key_concepts.definition: CRMs enforce strict deduplication. JobNimbus throws fatal errors on duplicate Display Names. The engine must append unique identifiers (job site number, timestamp, project ID).

key_concepts.name: Asynchronous Synchronization Failure
key_concepts.definition: Price sync latency between supplier catalogs and CRM product libraries (e.g., QBO-to-JobNimbus sync). The engine should cross-reference live supplier APIs as final validation.

entities.name: Xactimate Category Codes
entities.description: Standardized codes like RFG (roofing), SID (siding), DRY (drywall) used in insurance estimates

entities.name: Buildertrend
entities.description: CSV-based import via assembly templates

entities.name: JobNimbus
entities.description: REST API + QBO bridge for product import; strict deduplication rules

entities.name: AccuLynx
entities.description: REST API V2 with OAuth 2.0; partner access required for material order POST endpoints

entities.name: QuickBooks Online (QBO)
entities.description: Used as intermediary bridge for JobNimbus product/service imports

entities.name: ABC Supply
entities.description: National roofing supplier with AccuLynx native integration

entities.name: SRS Distribution
entities.description: National roofing supplier with AccuLynx native integration via Roof Hub

requirements.description: Lexicon Matrix must map all 50 core Xactimate codes to physical units and supplier equivalents
Dimensional analysis must handle pitch-adjusted calculations with configurable waste factors
Bundle rounding must ALWAYS round UP (never round down on physical materials)
O&P must be stripped from Xactimate totals before CRM injection
CSV generator must produce Buildertrend-compatible assembly import format
API adapter must handle AccuLynx OAuth 2.0 authentication flow
Deduplication logic must append unique identifiers to prevent CRM import errors
AccuLynx Material Order POST requires partner API access (undocumented in public API)

findings.finding: 50 core Xactimate codes map to a finite set of physical roofing materials
findings.evidence: Lexicon Matrix documented in source with specific code-to-material mappings

findings.finding: Xactimate prices are aggregate (material + labor + equipment + O&P) — cannot push directly to CRM cost fields
findings.evidence: AccuLynx API docs show separate Material Cost vs Retail Price fields; mixing causes negative margins

findings.finding: AccuLynx Material Order creation is a private/partner API — not publicly documented
findings.evidence: Public API docs focus on leads, contacts, webhooks, and financials only

findings.finding: JobNimbus has no native product import — requires QBO bridge as backdoor
findings.evidence: UserVoice feature request confirms community desire; QBO sync is documented workaround

findings.finding: Buildertrend assembly imports are the most accessible CSV-based path
findings.evidence: Published import template (XLSX) and documented field limits

documents.title: Data Friction Matrix Generation
documents.summary: Analyzes data friction points caused by the 'Human API' across industries (CRE, Construction, Logistics). Details the cost, business impact, and proposes AI/Code arbitrage solutions by replacing manual data translation with multimodal AI pipelines. Introduces the GOAS Assumption Triage Protocol.

key_concepts.name: Data Friction Matrix
key_concepts.definition: A structured analysis framework that maps: Source Format → Human Translator → Target Format, identifying cost, latency, error rate, and automation potential at each translation point.

key_concepts.name: Human API
key_concepts.definition: Humans acting as middleware translators between incompatible systems. The core bottleneck the Ontology Engine eliminates.

key_concepts.name: AI/Code Arbitrage
key_concepts.definition: Replacing expensive human translators with AI pipelines that can perform the same translation at lower cost, higher speed, and greater accuracy.

key_concepts.name: GOAS Assumption Triage Protocol
key_concepts.definition: A systematic process for identifying assumptions in a proposed solution, categorizing them by risk, and validating/killing them before implementation.

entities.name: Commercial Real Estate (CRE)
entities.description: Lease abstraction, CAM reconciliation, tenant billing workflows

entities.name: Construction (Residential)
entities.description: Xactimate to CRM translation for roofing contractors

entities.name: Logistics/Supply Chain
entities.description: BOL (Bill of Lading) processing, customs documentation

requirements.description: Data Friction Matrix must quantify cost-per-translation and error rate for each industry vertical
Assumption Triage Protocol must validate all critical assumptions before building

findings.finding: Human API bottleneck costs range from $25-150/hour across industries
findings.evidence: Salary analysis of data entry specialists, paralegals, abstractors in CRE, Construction, and Logistics

findings.finding: Error rates in manual translation range from 2-15% depending on complexity
findings.evidence: Industry reports on data entry accuracy in insurance claims processing

findings.finding: Multimodal AI can achieve 85-95% accuracy on structured data extraction with human review loop
findings.evidence: Benchmarks from OCR + LLM pipelines in document processing

documents.title: GOAS Data Friction Matrix Generation
documents.summary: Applies the GOAS framework to Data Friction Matrix analysis across three industry verticals: Custom Cabinetry, Legal Discovery, and Real Estate Appraisal. Each vertical is analyzed for automation opportunities where Human APIs can be replaced by AI/code solutions.

key_concepts.name: Custom Cabinetry Friction
key_concepts.definition: Translation bottleneck between cabinet design software (SketchUp/AutoCAD) and CNC machine G-code, currently mediated by skilled programmers.

key_concepts.name: Legal Discovery Friction
key_concepts.definition: Translation bottleneck between raw document dumps (emails, contracts, depositions) and structured review databases, currently mediated by paralegals/contract attorneys.

key_concepts.name: Real Estate Appraisal Friction
key_concepts.definition: Translation bottleneck between MLS data, property inspections, and MISMO XML-compliant appraisal forms (UAD 3.6), currently mediated by licensed appraisers.

entities.name: Custom Cabinetry
entities.description: Design-to-manufacturing pipeline requiring CAD → G-code translation

entities.name: Legal Discovery (eDiscovery)
entities.description: Document review pipeline requiring unstructured → structured translation

entities.name: Real Estate Appraisal
entities.description: Property valuation pipeline requiring field data → MISMO XML translation

entities.name: SketchUp
entities.description: 3D design software used in cabinetry

entities.name: AutoCAD
entities.description: CAD software used in manufacturing

entities.name: Relativity
entities.description: eDiscovery platform for legal document review

entities.name: MLS (Multiple Listing Service)
entities.description: Real estate listing database

requirements.description: Each industry vertical must have a complete friction matrix with quantified automation ROI

findings.finding: Custom cabinetry has a high-value friction point: skilled CNC programmers cost $60-100/hour and are in short supply
findings.evidence: Industry analysis of manufacturing labor costs and lead times

findings.finding: Legal discovery is a massive market ($15B+) with well-established eDiscovery platforms already using AI/ML
findings.evidence: Market analysis showing TAM and competitive landscape

findings.finding: Real estate appraisal automation is the highest-barrier but highest-moat opportunity due to GSE compliance requirements
findings.evidence: Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac UAD 3.6 mandate creates regulatory barrier to entry

documents.title: Ontology Engine SaaS — Strategic Summary
documents.summary: Comprehensive strategic summary synthesizing the Ontology Engine project vision: core thesis (monetizing the 'ontological divide' between ConTech and PropTech), GOAS methodology, competitive landscape analysis (verified white space), Phase 1/Phase 2 architecture, and data physics contracts (ingestion schema, supplement delta contract, UAD mapping logic).

key_concepts.name: Ontological Divide
key_concepts.definition: The market gap between the ConTech silo (contractor operations: CRMs, procurement) and the PropTech silo (mortgage compliance: UAD 3.6, MISMO XML). No existing competitor bridges both.

key_concepts.name: Competitive White Space
key_concepts.definition: Verified zero direct competitors bridging Xactimate parsing to UAD 3.6/MISMO 3.6 XML. ConTech players (CapOut.ai, EstimateHub, CRMs) lack PropTech mapping. PropTech players (ValueLink, Restb.ai) lack contractor intake.

key_concepts.name: Ingestion Contract Schema
key_concepts.definition: Formal JSON schema defining the canonical intake format: source_type (pdf|esx|email|scan|xactanalysis), customer PII fields, property/claim/estimate metadata, and confidence scores per extraction.

key_concepts.name: Supplement Delta Contract
key_concepts.definition: Schema for tracking changes between estimate versions: diffs with change_type (added|removed|qty_changed|price_changed|scope_changed), margin_impact, evidence_refs, and summary (added_rcv, removed_rcv, net_delta).

key_concepts.name: UAD Condition/Quality Mapping
key_concepts.definition: Logic to algorithmically map Xactimate Category/Selector codes to UAD 3.6 Condition Ratings (C1-C6) and Quality Ratings (Q1-Q6) for MISMO 3.6 XML export.

key_concepts.name: Pain Quantification
key_concepts.definition: Phase 1: 45-90 min per estimate, $1,050-$3,600/month at 40 packets/month. Phase 2: 60-120 min per appraisal, caps daily revenue capacity.

entities.name: CapOut.ai
entities.description: ConTech PDF parser/workflow tool. Strictly contractor-side; no UAD 3.6 mapping.

entities.name: EstimateHub / ClaimsFlow
entities.description: PDF-to-ESX converters within Xactimate ecosystem. No cross-industry translation.

entities.name: ValueLink / Appraise-It Pro
entities.description: PropTech appraisal ledgers/UAD translators. Rely on human appraiser workflow, don't ingest Xactimate.

entities.name: Restb.ai
entities.description: Visual AI property scanning. Guesses condition from photos; doesn't verify using contractor material lists.

entities.name: Canonical Claim Graph
entities.description: Internal structured representation that captures contact, claim metadata, estimate structure, worksheet sections, and material objects.

requirements.description: Ingestion must accept 5 source types: pdf, esx, email, scan, xactanalysis
Supplement tracking must support 5 change types in delta contracts
UAD mapping must produce C1-C6 condition and Q1-Q6 quality codes from Xactimate line items

findings.finding: Verified competitive white space: no player bridges ConTech (Xactimate parsing) to PropTech (UAD 3.6 MISMO XML)
findings.evidence: Analysis of CapOut.ai, EstimateHub, ValueLink, Restb.ai shows each is siloed in one vertical

findings.finding: Phase 1 pain is quantifiable: $1,050-$3,600/month per office in wasted labor for manual Xactimate-to-CRM data entry
findings.evidence: 45-90 min/estimate × 40 estimates/month = 30-60 hours/month of manual translation

findings.finding: Supplement delta tracking is a net-new capability not in the original PRD — enables version-aware estimate management
findings.evidence: Supplement delta contract schema defines change types, margin impact, and evidence references

findings.finding: The ingestion contract schema introduces multi-format intake (pdf, esx, email, scan, xactanalysis) beyond the initial PDF-only scope
findings.evidence: source_type field in ingestion contract schema

documents.title: Xactimate Data Extraction Research Plan
documents.summary: Exhaustive blueprint for building an OCR + semantic extraction pipeline for Xactimate PDF estimates. Covers macro-layout, micro-structure (9- vs 10-column tables), CAT/SEL/ACT taxonomy, ESX/XML target schemas, edge cases, and mathematical verification checkpoints.

key_concepts.name: Priority Asset Catalog
key_concepts.definition: 5 ranked, publicly available Xactimate PDF samples with direct URLs for parser training.

key_concepts.name: Dynamic Table Parsing
key_concepts.definition: OCR must detect 9-column (standard valuation) vs 10-column (action-oriented) layouts.

key_concepts.name: CAT/SEL/ACT Semantic Triangle
key_concepts.definition: Category Code + Selector Code + Activity Code: the universal taxonomy between contractors and insurers.

key_concepts.name: ESX Container Protocol
key_concepts.definition: .ESX files are renamed .zip archives containing structured XML (<XACTDOC> root).

key_concepts.name: Verification Checkpoints
key_concepts.definition: RCV equation, ACV equation, spatial variable reconciliation, schema compliance validation.

training_samples.name: UP Help Fire Loss
training_samples.value: Multi-page spanning, building code upgrade segregation

training_samples.name: Claims Delegates Draft
training_samples.value: 10-column layout, REMOVE/REPLACE split, Missing Wall deductions

training_samples.name: Assistimate Structure
training_samples.value: REDACTED string anomaly, coordinate sketch data

training_samples.name: Assistimate Contents
training_samples.value: Non-spatial units (HR, MO, EA), TBD costs

documents.title: GOAS Hybrid Architecture — Research Summary
documents.summary: Compact JSON summary defining the two-phase product strategy (Phase 1: The Wedge — Automated Parser, Phase 2: The Moat — Claims Database), competitive gaps, and Line_Item_Object schema with validation rules.

key_concepts.name: The Wedge (Phase 1)
key_concepts.definition: Automated Xactimate Parser: Ingest PDF/ESX -> Output Structured JSON.

key_concepts.name: The Moat (Phase 2)
key_concepts.definition: Proprietary Claims Database: Aggregated Line-Item Intelligence for benchmarking.

documents.title: Legal Risk Analysis: Xactimate ESX Data Extraction
documents.summary: Comprehensive legal risk analysis covering DMCA §1201, CFAA, EULA/contract risk, trade secret, antitrust, data portability, and industry precedent. Concludes EULA breach is highest risk; PDF/OCR is the legally safe path.

key_concepts.label: Anti-Circumvention (DMCA §1201)
key_concepts.definition: Federal prohibition on circumventing technological protection measures. Trivial obfuscation (rename .esx → .zip) likely does not qualify as a TPM per Lexmark v. Static Control.

key_concepts.label: Interoperability Exception (§1201(f))
key_concepts.definition: DMCA carve-out allowing reverse engineering solely to achieve interoperability between independently created programs.

key_concepts.label: EULA Restriction Risk
key_concepts.definition: Xactimate EULA likely prohibits reverse engineering. EULA breach is civil (injunctions, license revocation) and is the highest legal threat vector.

key_concepts.label: PDF-as-Safe-Harbor
key_concepts.definition: Strategy of processing only PDF exports via OCR. No circumvention, no format secrets, no EULA trap.

key_concepts.label: User-Origin Enforcement
key_concepts.definition: Mitigation pattern requiring users to certify file origin from their own licensed Xactimate instance.

findings.finding: EULA/contract breach is the highest legal risk vector
findings.evidence: EULA prohibits reverse engineering; violations lead to injunctions or license revocation

findings.finding: DMCA risk is Medium (ESX) / Low (PDF)
findings.evidence: Lexmark: trivial obfuscation not a TPM; Chamberlain: consumer right to use purchased software

findings.finding: CFAA risk is Low — processing user files is authorized
findings.evidence: Van Buren (2021) narrowed 'exceeds authorized access'; hiQ confirmed scraping isn't unauthorized

findings.finding: Trade secret risk is Low — ESX is trivially discoverable
findings.evidence: DTSA excludes reverse engineering by fair means; ZIP/XML has no reasonable secrecy

findings.finding: No known Verisk litigation against ESX parsers
findings.evidence: Industry scan found no C&D letters; authorized integrators (ClickClaims, Roofr) operate freely

findings.finding: PDF/OCR is the recommended safe path for MVP
findings.evidence: PDF export is expressly supported by Xactimate UI; no circumvention, no EULA trap

documents.title: GOAS Hybrid Architecture — Legal + Product Strategy
documents.summary: Strategic framework combining Z→A methodology with legal risk compression for ESX parsing. Defines two-phase product architecture (The Wedge: ESX→CRM parser; The Moat: aggregated pricing intelligence). Maps legal posture per phase using DMCA §1201(f), CFAA safe harbors, and FTC data portability. Includes refined data contracts with mathematical verification (PRICE*QTY+TAX+O&P=RCV) and strict BYOD user attestation.

key_concepts.label: The Wedge (Phase 1)
key_concepts.definition: Fast ESX-to-CRM parser that eliminates manual data entry. Strategic entry point for market penetration. Legal posture: DMCA §1201(f) interoperability exception + CFAA safe harbors.

key_concepts.label: The Moat (Phase 2)
key_concepts.definition: Aggregated anonymized pricing intelligence and material analytics across platforms. Creates defensible data ecosystem and workflow lock-in. Leverages FTC Right to Repair + state data portability frameworks.

key_concepts.label: BYOD (Bring Your Own Data)
key_concepts.definition: Core legal and product principle requiring users to supply their own locally-saved ESX files via affirmative warranty of data ownership. No credential sharing, no server scraping.

key_concepts.label: Legal Risk Compression
key_concepts.definition: Strategy of reducing multi-vector legal exposure to near-zero by staying outside authentication gates, avoiding trademark use, and relying on federal copyright preemption.

key_concepts.label: Mathematical Verification Protocol
key_concepts.definition: Algorithmic reconciliation: (PRICE * QTY) + TAX + O&P = RCV. Must match Grand Total Areas before CRM dispatch. Core quality gate for Node 3.

key_concepts.label: Dynamic Tabular Mapping
key_concepts.definition: Output transformation handling both 9-column valuation layouts and 10-column Remove/Replace action layouts from Xactimate.

findings.finding: CFAA threat is zero — operations occur entirely outside Verisk authentication gates on user-provided local files
findings.evidence: hiQ Labs v. LinkedIn; Van Buren v. United States

findings.finding: DMCA threat is low — renaming .esx to .zip is trivial obfuscation, failing the 'effective TPM' standard
findings.evidence: Lexmark v. Static Control

findings.finding: EULA tortious interference is medium-high — mitigated by federal copyright preemption and trademark abstention
findings.evidence: No Verisk trademark use, no affiliation claims

findings.finding: Phase 2 (The Moat) provides legal deterrent via FTC Right to Repair and emerging data portability mandates
findings.evidence: FTC open data mandates, state-level bills like Hawaii SB 1037

findings.finding: Two layout formats exist in Xactimate output: 9-column valuation and 10-column Remove/Replace action
findings.evidence: Data extraction research confirms dual-layout requirement

relationships.detail: Provides strategic legal framing building on DOC_010 risk analysis
Operationalizes Z→A pipeline methodology with legal posture
Refines GOAS architecture with two-phase product strategy
Specifies CRM integration targets from Blueprint

nodes.label: Ontology Engine
nodes.definition: Middleware that automates translation of analog/unstructured data into structured, system-ready formats

nodes.label: Z → A Pipeline
nodes.definition: Reverse extrapolation methodology: define output (Z) first, work backward to input (A)

nodes.label: GOAS
nodes.definition: Goal-Oriented Action System — planning framework that works backward from goals to prerequisites

nodes.label: Human API
nodes.definition: Humans acting as middleware translators between incompatible systems — the core bottleneck

nodes.label: Design by Contract
nodes.definition: Formal agreements (preconditions, postconditions, invariants) enforced at every node boundary via JSON schema

nodes.label: Agentic V-Model
nodes.definition: Acceptance criteria and tests defined BEFORE generative execution

nodes.label: 4-Node Pipeline
nodes.definition: Ingestion → Extraction → Calculus → Routing — the core processing chain

nodes.label: Ingestion Node
nodes.definition: Node 1: Accepts data, performs OCR if needed, redacts PII

nodes.label: Semantic Extraction Node
nodes.definition: Node 2: LLM extracts raw variables into rigid JSON schema. LLMs NEVER calculate.

nodes.label: Deterministic Calculus Node
nodes.definition: Node 3: Python applies industry-specific math (dimensional analysis, waste factors, rounding) to convert abstract units into physical SKUs

nodes.label: Output Routing Node
nodes.definition: Node 4: Transforms structured JSON into target-specific formats (CSV, JSON, MISMO XML) via APIs or CSV generators

nodes.label: Lexicon Matrix
nodes.definition: Mapping of ~50 Xactimate codes to physical units, material categories, and supplier equivalents

nodes.label: Roofer Math
nodes.definition: Deterministic conversion formulas: Squares→Bundles(×3), LF→pieces(÷10), pitch waste factors, always-round-UP

nodes.label: Data Friction Matrix
nodes.definition: Maps Source→Translator→Target to quantify cost, latency, and error of human translation

nodes.label: AI/Code Arbitrage
nodes.definition: Replacing expensive human translators with AI pipelines at lower cost and higher accuracy

nodes.label: PII Redaction
nodes.definition: All personally identifiable information must be masked locally before LLM processing

nodes.label: O&P Double-Dip Prevention
nodes.definition: Overhead & Profit must be stripped from Xactimate totals before CRM injection

nodes.label: UAD 3.6 MISMO XML
nodes.definition: Uniform Appraisal Dataset v3.6 — GSE-mandated format for real estate appraisals

nodes.label: Competitive White Space
nodes.definition: Verified gap: no competitor bridges ConTech (Xactimate parsing) to PropTech (UAD 3.6 MISMO XML)

nodes.label: Supplement Delta Contract
nodes.definition: Schema for tracking changes between estimate versions with typed diffs (added/removed/qty_changed/price_changed/scope_changed)

nodes.label: Ingestion Contract Schema
nodes.definition: Canonical intake JSON format supporting 5 source types (pdf, esx, email, scan, xactanalysis) with customer, property, claim, estimate fields

nodes.label: UAD Condition/Quality Mapping
nodes.definition: Logic to convert Xactimate Category/Selector codes to UAD 3.6 Condition (C1-C6) and Quality (Q1-Q6) ratings for MISMO XML

nodes.label: DMCA Anti-Circumvention
nodes.definition: Federal prohibition on circumventing TPMs. Key question: does trivial ESX obfuscation qualify?

nodes.label: EULA Contract Risk
nodes.definition: Risk of Xactimate license violation from parsing proprietary formats. Highest-severity legal threat.

nodes.label: PDF Safe Harbor Strategy
nodes.definition: Use PDF/OCR as primary ingestion to avoid DMCA and EULA risk. ESX deferred.

nodes.label: Interoperability Defense
nodes.definition: DMCA §1201(f) exception for reverse engineering to achieve program interoperability.

nodes.label: User-Origin Attestation
nodes.definition: Mitigation pattern: users certify they exported files from their own Xactimate license.

nodes.label: The Wedge (Phase 1 Market Entry)
nodes.definition: Fast ESX-to-CRM parser for immediate workflow friction reduction. First-mover advantage via speed and accuracy.

nodes.label: The Moat (Phase 2 Data Ecosystem)
nodes.definition: Aggregated anonymized pricing intelligence creating defensible data ecosystem. Contractors and CRMs become dependent on the intelligence layer.

nodes.label: BYOD Legal Model
nodes.definition: Bring-Your-Own-Data: users must warrant ownership of files being processed. Core legal shield eliminating CFAA and reducing EULA exposure.

nodes.label: Dual Layout Detection
nodes.definition: Xactimate produces two output layouts: 9-column valuation and 10-column Remove/Replace action. Parser must handle both dynamically.

nodes.label: Federal Copyright Preemption
nodes.definition: Defensive strategy using federal copyright preemption to counter EULA tortious interference claims. Avoids Verisk trademark use.

preconditions: File is a valid PDF or image format
postconditions: OCR confidence score attached to each text block
invariants: Zero PII in output — no exceptions

nodes.name: Semantic Extraction Node
outputs: Structured JSON matching rigid extraction schema

preconditions: Input text is within LLM context window (or chunked)
postconditions: Output JSON validates against defined schema
All Xactimate codes from input are present in output
NO calculations performed — raw values only
invariants: LLM never invents data not present in source

nodes.name: Deterministic Calculus Node
outputs: Calculated JSON with physical units, quantities, and costs

preconditions: All required fields present (area, pitch, code)
postconditions: All quantities converted to physical units (bundles, pieces, rolls)
invariants: No floating-point rounding errors in currency

preconditions: Input JSON passes calculated schema validation
Target CRM identified and adapter available
postconditions: Output matches target CRM import format exactly
invariants: Data integrity preserved end-to-end from Node 1 input to Node 4 output

workstreams.name: Workstream Alpha: Ingestion, OCR & Sanitization

tasks.description: Build OCR router (Tesseract for scanned, direct parse for native PDF)
Implement PII redaction pipeline using Microsoft Presidio
Build token chunking strategy for large documents
Acquire 5+ diverse Xactimate PDF samples for testing

workstreams.acceptance_criteria: OCR produces clean text from both native and scanned PDFs with PII fully redacted. Token chunking handles documents exceeding LLM context windows.

workstreams.name: Workstream Beta: Ontology Engine Core

tasks.description: Define rigid JSON extraction schema for Xactimate data
Engineer LLM prompt for semantic extraction (no calculation)
Build Roofer Math Python module (Squares→Bundles, pitch adjustment, waste factors)
Implement F9 note collision detection with HITL gate

workstreams.acceptance_criteria: LLM extracts all Xactimate codes into valid JSON without performing any calculations. Python module correctly converts all values to physical units with proper rounding, O&P handling, and credit_return flagging.

workstreams.name: Workstream Gamma: Destination Adapters

tasks.description: Build Buildertrend CSV generator (assembly import format)
Build JobNimbus QBO bridge CSV generator
Research AccuLynx partner API access for Material Order POST
Build deduplication logic for CRM imports

workstreams.acceptance_criteria: CSV generators produce valid import files for Buildertrend and JobNimbus. AccuLynx API research completed with partner access path documented.

workstreams.name: Workstream Delta: Phase 2 R&D

tasks.description: Research UAD 3.6 MISMO XML schema specifications
Identify GSE submission API requirements for Fannie Mae / Freddie Mac
Map appraisal data friction points to Ontology Engine architecture

workstreams.acceptance_criteria: UAD 3.6 schema fully documented. GSE submission requirements documented. Architecture adaptation plan drafted.

hardcoded_rules.name: PII Redaction
hardcoded_rules.description: All personally identifiable information (names, addresses, SSNs, policy numbers) must be masked by local Python script (Microsoft Presidio) BEFORE any data reaches the LLM.
hardcoded_rules.mitigation_strategy: Microsoft Presidio runs as first step in Node 1. Zero-data-retention policy for LLM API calls.

hardcoded_rules.name: Tax & O&P Double-Dip Prevention
hardcoded_rules.description: Xactimate estimates include Overhead & Profit margins. CRMs apply their own O&P. Node 3 must strip O&P from Xactimate totals before CRM injection to prevent double-taxation.
hardcoded_rules.mitigation_strategy: Deterministic Calculus Node (Node 3) explicitly strips O&P line items and recalculates material-only costs.

hardcoded_rules.name: Negative Quantity Handling
hardcoded_rules.description: Negative integers in Xactimate supplements represent credits/deductions. These must be flagged as 'credit_return' transactions, not treated as errors.
hardcoded_rules.mitigation_strategy: Node 3 detects negative quantities and routes them to a credit_return data path with separate CRM handling.

hardcoded_rules.name: F9 Note Collision
hardcoded_rules.description: Xactimate F9 notes can override standard line item values with adjuster-specific modifications. LLM must flag these but NEVER auto-resolve the conflict.
hardcoded_rules.mitigation_strategy: Node 2 (LLM) flags F9 note collisions. Human-in-the-Loop gate requires manual approval before overridden values propagate.

hardcoded_rules.name: Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) Gate
hardcoded_rules.description: Any output with confidence below threshold or F9 note conflicts must be held for human review before final CRM submission.
hardcoded_rules.mitigation_strategy: HITL queue system with notification. User approves/rejects/edits before data proceeds to Node 4.

hardcoded_rules.name: PDF-First Ingestion (Legal Safe Harbor)
hardcoded_rules.description: MVP must use PDF/OCR extraction only. ESX/XML parsing blocked pending legal counsel review of Xactimate EULA.
hardcoded_rules.mitigation_strategy: Node 1 already supports PDF via pdfplumber. ESX path deferred.

hardcoded_rules.name: User-Origin Enforcement
hardcoded_rules.description: All files must be user-exported from their own licensed Xactimate. Require certification of file origin.
hardcoded_rules.mitigation_strategy: Upload flow with attestation checkbox + timestamp logging.

hardcoded_rules.name: No Format Spec Distribution
hardcoded_rules.description: Never publish any reverse-engineered ESX/XML format specs. Protects DMCA §1201(f) defense.
hardcoded_rules.mitigation_strategy: Parsing logic in private repo. No public documentation of ESX structure.

hardcoded_rules.name: RCV Reconciliation Gate
hardcoded_rules.description: Before CRM dispatch, algorithmically verify: (PRICE * QTY) + TAX + O&P = RCV. Must match the document's Grand Total Areas. Reject if mismatch exceeds tolerance.
hardcoded_rules.mitigation_strategy: Node 3 roofer_math already computes adjusted totals. Add reconciliation check against extracted totals from Node 2.

risk_backlog.name: PII & Data Privacy Liability
risk_backlog.description: Processing insurance/appraisal documents may expose PII regulated by HIPAA, state privacy laws, and insurance regulations.
risk_backlog.mitigation_strategy: Local PII redaction before LLM. Zero-data-retention agreements with LLM providers. Legal review of data handling policies.

risk_backlog.name: API Paywall Trap
risk_backlog.description: CRM API access may require Premium/Enterprise subscription tiers (e.g., AccuLynx partner access). Contractors on lower tiers may not have API access.
risk_backlog.mitigation_strategy: Audit all target CRM subscription tiers for API availability. Fall back to CSV import where API unavailable.

risk_backlog.name: Context Window / Token Limit Drop-off
risk_backlog.description: Large Xactimate PDFs may exceed LLM context windows, causing information loss or degraded extraction accuracy.
risk_backlog.mitigation_strategy: Token chunking strategy with overlap windows. Cost analysis for multi-call processing. May need document segmentation by trade.

risk_backlog.name: OCR Volatility
risk_backlog.description: Scanned PDFs vs native PDFs produce vastly different text quality. Dynamic OCR router needed.
risk_backlog.mitigation_strategy: Implement PDF type detection. Native PDFs use direct text extraction. Scanned PDFs route through Tesseract/AWS Textract with confidence scoring.

risk_backlog.name: Multi-Trade Routing Collision
risk_backlog.description: A single Xactimate estimate may contain multiple trades (roofing, siding, gutters) destined for different suppliers. Data must be split correctly.
risk_backlog.mitigation_strategy: Xactimate category codes naturally segment by trade. Node 2 extracts trade tags. Node 3 splits by trade for separate routing in Node 4.

risk_backlog.name: EULA Breach from ESX Parsing
risk_backlog.description: Verisk EULA likely prohibits reverse engineering of software output. Primary litigation weapon.

risk_backlog.name: DMCA §1201 Circumvention Claim
risk_backlog.description: Decoding ESX format could trigger anti-circumvention claims despite trivial obfuscation.

risk_backlog.name: Verisk Enforcement Action
risk_backlog.description: No known lawsuits against ESX parsers, but Verisk may enforce quietly via C&D.
risk_backlog.mitigation_strategy: CONST_008 + monitor industry + legal counsel on retainer

risk_backlog.name: Tortious Interference via EULA
risk_backlog.description: Verisk could claim tortious interference with contractual relations (EULA). Distinct from direct EULA breach (RISK_003).
risk_backlog.mitigation_strategy: Federal copyright preemption + zero Verisk trademark usage + no affiliation claims. BYOD model (CONST_007).

prd_synthesis.problem_statement: Skilled professionals currently act as 'Human APIs' — manually translating unstructured data (Xactimate estimates, blueprints, legal documents, real estate appraisals) into structured formats required by downstream systems (CRMs, ERPs, procurement platforms). This creates significant latency (hours to days per translation), high cost ($25-150/hour), and error cascades (2-15% error rates). The Ontology Engine eliminates this bottleneck by automating the full translation pipeline.

target_markets.rationale: Fastest time-to-revenue. Clear pain point, existing contractor relationships. Three target CRMs (JobNimbus, AccuLynx, Buildertrend) with documented integration paths.

target_markets.market: Real Estate Appraisers (Field Data → UAD 3.6 MISMO XML)
target_markets.rationale: Higher enterprise value and competitive moat due to GSE certification requirements. Longer development timeline.

system_architecture.pattern: 4-Node Z→A Pipeline with Design by Contract

success_metrics: Process an Xactimate PDF → CRM-ready output in < 60 seconds
Achieve 95%+ extraction accuracy on structured fields
Successful import/API push to at least 2 of 3 target CRMs
Contractor time savings: from 30-60 min manual entry to < 2 min automated

alpha.name: Node 1: Ingestion (OCR + PII Redaction)
alpha.mode_rationale: Multiple interconnected components (OCR routing, PII pipeline, chunking), design decisions required, needs to download/integrate test fixtures

beta.name: Nodes 2+3: Semantic Extraction + Deterministic Calculus
beta.mode_rationale: Complex domain logic (Lexicon Matrix, roofer math, O&P stripping), multiple modules with dependencies

gamma.name: Node 4: Output Routing (CRM Adapters)
gamma.mode_rationale: CRM integration nuances (JobNimbus duplicate errors, AccuLynx private API research), format decisions per CRM target

delta.name: Phase 2 R&D: UAD 3.6 / MISMO XML
delta.mode_rationale: Pure research workstream requiring deep investigation of UAD 3.6 / MISMO XML standards

tasks.title: Create SKILL.md
Build preflight.py
Build kb.py
Build dashboard.py
Build compile_briefing.py
Build validate_kb.py
Create resources + examples
Verify all scripts
Wire pipeline.py (chain Nodes 1→4 with contract validation)
Implement hitl/review_queue.py (local JSON queue)
Add CLI entry point __main__.py
Write integration tests for pipeline + HITL
Add google-generativeai dependency
Wire extractor to Gemini Flash

tasks.rationale: Ralph pattern — agents append learnings to a persistent file so future sessions benefit from discovered patterns, gotchas, and conventions. Each agent iteration appends, never overwrites.

tasks.title: Add reasoning_level field to KB workstream tasks
tasks.rationale: PRD Planner pattern — each task gets a reasoning level (1-5) hinting which model/mode to use. Low = fast/mechanical, High = planning/novel. Helps the orchestrator recommend session mode.

tasks.title: Add skill field to KB workstream tasks for skill-to-task mapping
tasks.rationale: PRD Implementation Planning pattern — each task names which installed skill should execute it. Enables the agent to auto-load the right skill when starting a task.

tasks.title: Cross-reference kb-synthesizer and kb-orchestrator in their SKILL.md files
tasks.rationale: Users and agents need to understand the pipeline: synthesize → orchestrate → agent → done. Both skills should reference each other explicitly.

tasks.title: Update validate_kb.py to check new fields (reasoning_level, skill)
tasks.rationale: Validator should warn if tasks are missing the new optional fields so KBs stay rich.

tasks.title: Define Node 5 input/output contracts (two Node 3 outputs → gap report schema)
Implement line-item diff engine (missing items, quantity deltas, pricing deltas)
Implement O&P detection (trade count → warranted O&P vs applied O&P)
Implement depreciation audit (age/life ratio validation)
Write unit tests using McKee case study data from supplement_analysis
Define supplement report output schema (structured JSON → human-readable report)
Implement gap-to-narrative formatter (category-by-category breakdown)
Implement dollar-impact summarizer (per-category and total recovery estimate)
Wire into pipeline.py (adjuster PDF + contractor PDF → Nodes 1-3 × 2 → Node 5 → Node 6 → Node 4)
Write integration tests using McKee case study (verify $60K gap detection)

kilo.name: Red Team Suite (Adversarial Testing)
kilo.mode_rationale: Adversarial test design requires creative thinking about failure modes across all 6 nodes + pipeline

tasks.title: Malformed PDF adversarial tests (Node 1 — corrupt headers, zero-byte, password-protected, 500-page stress)
LLM extraction edge cases (Node 2 — gibberish text, prompt injection attempts, empty/null responses, partial JSON)
Calculus overflow/underflow (Node 3 — extreme values, division by zero, negative quantities, NaN propagation)
Contract boundary fuzzing (all node boundaries — invalid schemas, missing required fields, type coercion attacks)
Node 5+6 adversarial (identical estimates, empty gap reports, extreme deltas, single-line-item edge cases)
End-to-end pipeline stress (corrupted mid-pipeline, partial node failures, HITL queue overflow, timeout simulation)

lima.name: UI Shell (Web Frontend)
lima.mode_rationale: Full-stack UI requires design decisions on framework, layout, component architecture, and API surface

PDF upload component (drag-and-drop, dual-file for adjuster + contractor, validation)
Pipeline status dashboard (real-time node progress, contract validation indicators)
Supplement report viewer (render Node 6 output — gap table, O&P analysis, dollar-impact summary)
HITL review interface (list flagged items, approve/reject actions, queue status)

M5: API endpoint tests (upload, rate limit, kill switch, error handling)

N2: Wire report/review views to /api/history endpoints (list + detail)

papa.name: App Documentation
tasks: P1: README.md — project overview, architecture diagram, quick start, deployment guide
P2: docs/APP_STATUS.md — current state, what works, what is planned
P3: docs/FINANCIAL.md — cost analysis, pricing models, monetization strategy

case_study.job: McKee, Charles — 2355 Round Top Dr, Honolulu

op_recovery.description: Adjuster applied 0% O&P. Contractor applied 10%/10%. 9 trades involved = O&P absolutely warranted.

missing_items.description: 24 line items in contractor scope not in adjuster scope. Includes dust barriers, pool tarping, emergency calls, floor protection, insulation, can light D&R.

quantity_discrepancies.description: 10+ items where adjuster undercount >20%. Mod bit roof +145%, hip/ridge +106%, drip edge +42%, drywall +102%.

depreciation_audit.description: Adjuster depreciated paint at 46.67%, carpet at 70%. Recoverable upon completion but worth auditing age/life ratios.

product_pivot.to: Supplement intelligence — two-PDF comparison + automated gap detection + supplement report generation
product_pivot.architecture: Both PDFs through Nodes 1-3 independently → new Node 5 (Comparator) → new Node 6 (Supplement Report) → existing Node 4 (CRM Export)

pdfs_on_disk: tests/McKee. Charles 2355 Round Top Drive Xactimate.pdf

learnings.insight: difflib.SequenceMatcher provides sufficient fuzzy matching for Xactimate line-item descriptions without external deps (>=80% threshold avoids false misses)
O&P recovery is the highest-value single gap category — McKee case showed ~.6K (20% of K RCV) from a single missing 10/10 application on 9 trades
Node 5 input contract reuses NODE_3_TO_4 shape via JSON Schema $defs, keeping contracts DRY — Agent J can directly reference NODE_5_OUTPUT_SCHEMA for its input
Node 6 supplement reports are fully deterministic (no LLM calls) — all narratives are template-based, making them suitable for programmatic carrier submission

The supplement report pipeline is: Node5 gap_report → format_category_narratives → summarize_financials → format_op_narrative → format_depreciation_narrative → build_recommended_actions → assemble report. Each step is independently testable.
Presidio US_SSN recognizer misses SSNs when prefixed with label text like 'SSN:' — consider running regex as secondary detection pass
Presidio email detection may fail in very long text (28K+ chars) — chunk text before PII analysis for reliability
_parse_llm_response returns None for 'null' JSON literal — downstream functions must guard against None return
Vite create-vite CLI uses relative paths from CWD — must cd into target directory first, not pass absolute path
HTML disabled attribute is boolean — setting it to any value (including undefined via setAttribute) disables the element. Must conditionally add/remove, not set to undefined
Observable state store with subscribe/setState pattern works well for vanilla JS — no framework needed. Keep state flat for simple re-render triggers.
FastAPI TestClient uses 'testclient' as default client.host — rate limiter IP detection uses this in tests
run_supplement_pipeline can be implemented inline in api.py to avoid editing shared pipeline.py — calls _run_nodes_1_to_3 twice then Node 5 + Node 6 directly

learnings.title: Supabase Integration Pivot
learnings.detail: Pivoted from access-code-gate + in-memory state to full Supabase integration. Supabase handles: Auth (email/password + magic link), PostgreSQL (analyses + rate_limits tables with RLS), Storage (estimates bucket for PDFs). FastAPI backend still needed for compute (pdfplumber, Gemini, etc). Project: ontology-engine (fareqnzxhodvgdkboeff) on Adair AI org, us-west-1, free tier. Frontend uses @supabase/supabase-js, backend uses supabase-py + PyJWT. Auth is gracefully disabled when SUPABASE_URL not set (backward-compatible).

learnings.insight: API response has both gap_report and report top-level keys; frontend must map from gap_report.summary for the report view since that contains the Node 5 output shape
FormData for multipart uploads must NOT set Content-Type header manually — fetch auto-sets it with the boundary
